current_objective.title: Expand input handling, texture workflows, and platform capability coverage
success_evidence: Input handling is expanded with deterministic validation evidence
Texture workflows cover load/swap/fallback/error-path behavior across representative scenarios
Platform-capability matrix evidence is updated for the expanded input/texture slice
Validation gates remain green after phase-5 implementation
orchestration_evidence: phase-4 objective closed with stdlib/runtime evidence and QA pass
phase-3 performance clean kickoff now running on codex/perf-phase3-clean-start with tracker/state synchronized before optimization patch intake
follow_through_order: runtime-draw-flush-batch slice implementation and evidence capture
example-scenario-worker in parallel with playable feature scenarios for manual user validation
phase_pending_checklist: R3-A: freeze 2-run direct before/after summaries at 25000 with 2.5-second sessions and stack metadata
R3-B: execute parallel optimization slices and keep only patches with positive quick-protocol delta
R3-C: verify no >5% regression at 3000/6000 before checkpoint commit

tasks.title: Validate and close the infrastructure bootstrap
evidence: Directory cleaned
Git initialized on codex/bootstrap-workspace
Governance docs and ADR log created
Cargo workspace scaffolded and later simplified to single-crate pycro_cli with module boundaries
Canonical stub file generated at python/pycro/__init__.pyi
cargo fmt --all --check passed
python3 scripts/validate_governance.py passed
cargo metadata --format-version 1 --locked --no-deps passed
cargo test passed
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed
cargo run --bin generate_stubs -- --check python/pycro/__init__.pyi passed
python3 -m mypy --config-file pyproject.toml passed
qa-reviewer outcome recorded: pass for phase-0 closeout; no technical gate failures remain
docs-tracker synced tracker/state for phase handoff to phase-1
phase-1 handoff approved: runtime-basic-example-loader is the next active task
tasks.next_action: Phase 0 closed. Hand off to runtime-basic-example-loader as the active phase-1 task.

tasks.title: Maintain Mermaid visualizations for lifecycle and dispatch
evidence: docs/flow-mermaids.md maintained by flow-visualizer as a cross-cutting documentation stream
Diagrams refreshed for lifecycle, dispatch, delivery, and phase closeout transitions
Phase-5 diagrams now capture draw-batch queueing during update(dt), single flush_draw_batch() dispatch per frame, and discard-on-update-failure behavior
flow-mermaid refresh was merged to main via commit 930bc64
tasks.next_action: Reopen this continuous stream on the next lifecycle/API/dispatch contract change.

tasks.title: Load a real script path and dispatch setup plus update(dt) through runtime module
evidence: RustPythonVm introduced with persistent interpreter and real script loading
Runtime installs pycro module aliases and lifecycle callable surface inside RustPython
setup()/update(dt) dispatch execute on real script path with Python stdout flush
tasks.next_action: Improve RustPython exception/report formatting in the next runtime slice.

tasks.title: Provide the first desktop Macroquad loop and backend contract implementation
evidence: DesktopFrameLoop + FrameLoopConfig introduced as desktop loop owner boundary
CLI execution now runs update(dt) inside Macroquad's real window/event loop ownership
MacroquadBackendContract executes and records backend dispatch operations for smoke evidence
tasks.next_action: Expand input mapping and texture asset resolution coverage across platforms.

tasks.title: Connect the public pycro API registry to the backend contract without breaking stub generation
evidence: Runtime pycro module now queues typed API operations in module state
Runtime drains queue and dispatches render/assets/camera calls into EngineBackend
Stub generator and metadata contract remain unchanged and validated
tasks.next_action: Upgrade bridge dispatch to direct callable backend bindings with richer return-path semantics.

tasks.title: Prove that examples/basic_main.py executes on the real desktop path
evidence: cargo run -- examples/basic_main.py passes
setup() runs once and update(dt) executes through Macroquad loop owner
backend api dispatch count is emitted as smoke evidence
PYCRO_FRAMES=2 cargo run -- examples/basic_main.py validates non-simulated deterministic execution
tasks.next_action: Add CI-friendly screenshot/assertion smoke for render payloads.

tasks.title: Replace queued API operation dispatch with direct backend bridge and rich return semantics
evidence: Objective and phase activated in tracker/state with branch aligned to codex/api-direct-bridge-rich-returns

Runtime installs direct RustPython callables for pycro API and removes queue-drain dispatch path
draw_text API added across metadata/runtime/backend and ADR 0005 created
examples flattened to examples/*.py with shared examples/assets and stopwatch_seconds.py scenario
example-scenario-worker policy now defaults texture scenarios to the Kenney shared pack and requires explicit asset-path documentation
Added pack-focused playable scenarios (checkerboard_pan.py, gradient_cycle.py, noise_scanner.py) using examples/assets/kenney_development_essentials
python3 scripts/validate_governance.py passed
python3 -m mypy --config-file pyproject.toml passed
target/debug/generate_stubs --check python/pycro/__init__.pyi passed

tasks.title: Guarantee local Python module imports from the entry script directory
evidence: Phase-3 objective activated for entry-script local import compatibility

runtime::tests::load_main_supports_importing_sidecar_python_modules_from_script_directory passed

evidence: Phase-4 activated after phase-3 import compatibility closeout
ADR 0007 accepted for runtime stdlib compatibility contract (`math`, `os`) while keeping sidecar precedence
runtime::tests::load_main_supports_stdlib_math_and_os_imports passed
runtime::tests::load_main_prefers_sidecar_module_over_stdlib_module_name_collision passed
runtime::tests::load_main_supports_importing_sidecar_python_modules_from_script_directory stayed green
PYCRO_FRAMES=3 cargo run -- examples/stdlib_math_os.py passed with direct stdlib imports
tasks.next_action: begin next objective on expanded input/textures/platform coverage and CI visual/payload assertion smoke.

tasks.title: Expand input handling, texture workflows, and platform capability coverage
evidence: Phase-5 activated with dedicated branch codex/platform-input-texture-coverage after phase-4 closeout
Active execution slice moved to branch codex/runtime-draw-flush-batch for runtime draw batching and frame flush integration
Runtime now queues draw operations during update(dt) and flushes once per frame from main loop via runtime.flush_draw_batch()
runtime::tests::draw_ops_are_queued_in_batch_order_until_frame_flush and runtime::tests::draw_batch_flush_clears_per_frame added as regression guards
PYCRO_FRAMES=3 cargo run -- examples/runtime_draw_flush_batch.py passes with per-frame draw-call logs and backend dispatch evidence
Controlled benchmark comparison versus baseline branch shows no improvement at target loads: 3000 balls = 20.0 FPS and 4000 balls = 20.0 FPS on both branches in current protocol
PYCRO_PERF=1 telemetry indicates high-load bottleneck is dominated by Python update cost (~103 ms/frame) versus draw flush (~12.6 ms/frame) at ~4009 dispatches/frame near 4000 balls
Benchmark integrity rule added in docs/validation-policy.md and docs/agent-playbook.md to prevent false-positive gains from moving user gameplay loops into Rust
qa-reviewer finding resolved: on update failure runtime discards queued draw batch; regression guard runtime::tests::update_failure_discards_queued_draw_batch added
Public API now includes submit_render(commands) with batch parsing/queueing in runtime and parity guard runtime::tests::submit_render_matches_legacy_draw_path_order_and_payload
ADR 0009 accepted for submit_render batch API contract and source-of-truth stub updates
Short benchmark protocol standardized to 3000 initial balls and 3-second auto sessions with wall_fps as canonical metric
Release benchmark evidence: pycro remains stable above 60 wall_fps at 3000 and 6000 balls in short auto runs
Phase-3 baseline at 25000 (direct, 5s) improved pycro from ~19-20 to ~22 wall_fps after first runtime+benchmark optimization slice; still below pygame-ce reference
macroquad-direct 25000 benchmark reached ~112-117 wall_fps (user observed ~80.37), confirming macroquad backend is not the bottleneck
Direct 3-second baseline refresh at 25000: pycro=23.29 wall_fps, pygame-ce(.venv314)=33.37 wall_fps, macroquad_direct=81.16 wall_fps
Phase-3 execution plan captured in docs/perf-phase-3-plan.md with parallel subagent ownership and before/after median gates
Clean kickoff on codex/perf-phase3-clean-start removed stale examples/compare/results* artifacts before new baseline capture
Clean kickoff deployed three investigators in parallel (runtime hotspot, harness audit, measurement auditor) and consolidated risks around noisy outliers, protocol drift, and metadata incompleteness

Direct 25000/3s baseline captured as 3x runs per runtime with current summary medians: pycro≈23.83 wall_fps, pygame-ce(.venv314)≈40.00 wall_fps, macroquad_direct≈77.28 wall_fps (pygame includes one noisy outlier run)
One pygame-ce direct run reported elapsed≈3.84 with summary wall_fps=0.00 and is tagged as noisy data pending repeat/filter per protocol

Optimization slice S1 applied in runtime submit_render path (borrowed PyStr command-name parse + direct draw_batch enqueue with rollback, removing temporary ops Vec) to reduce per-frame allocation/parsing overhead

S1 quick 25000/3s direct pycro check (3x) produced summary wall_fps 24.16 / 24.07 / 23.86 (median≈24.07) versus clean-start summary median≈23.83; provisional +0.24 wall_fps pending sample-normalized acceptance gate + 3000/6000 guardrail reruns

Optimization slice S2 updated Rust runtime internals with QueuedDrawBatch circle compaction (CircleRun), cached submit_render command storage behind Arc, a cached-position Vec2 fast path for draw_circle, cached setup()/update(dt) callables after load_main(), and configurable per-update stdio flushing with default off
Phase-3 quick benchmark decision protocol now uses direct 25000-ball runs with 2.5-second sessions and 2 runs per candidate before 3000/6000 guardrail verification
Latest slice-2 direct pycro summaries at 25000 recorded wall_fps 25.43 / 25.09 / 25.06 / 24.57, improving over the prior pre-slice-2 baseline band of roughly 22.5-23.4 wall_fps

Direct freeze protocol rerun on codex/docs-flow-mermaid-views (25000 balls, 2.5s, 2 runs each) captured wall_fps medians: pycro≈24.53 (22.21/26.84), pygame-ce(.venv314)≈40.24 (40.08/40.40), macroquad_direct≈78.64 (75.56/81.72)
Runtime stack metadata captured with the freeze: host=Darwin arm64, rustc=1.91.1, cargo=1.91.1, system python=3.9.5, pygame-ce stack python=3.14.2 + pygame-ce=2.5.7 + SDL=2.32.10

Guardrails rerun (2.5s, 2 runs) stayed strong: 3000 balls wall_fps 120.03/117.97 (median≈119.00) and 6000 balls wall_fps 110.05/112.12 (median≈111.09), no >5% regression signal in this checkpoint sample
Phase-3 closeout backlog and future proposals documented in docs/perf-phase-3-closeout.md, including explicit reminder to extend circle vector->sprite optimizations to remaining geometric primitives
tasks.next_action: keep the 25000/2.5s 2-run quick protocol for patch triage, rerun 3000/6000 guardrails before acceptance, and record full stack/session metadata on every retained comparison.

tasks.title: Compare pycro and pygame with equivalent bouncing-ball load benchmark scenarios
evidence: Isolated benchmark stream activated in parallel to roadmap phases
examples/compare_pycro_balls_benchmark.py added with per-second FPS sampling and Left/Right controls
examples/compare_pygame_balls_benchmark.py added with equivalent simulation and HUD contract
examples/compare/README.md added with run protocol and results template
Both compare scripts now emit homogeneous logs with stable/unstable status, stable_sample_seconds, and nearest target tracking for configured targets
Auto benchmark defaults now use short protocol and BENCHMARK_AUTO_INITIAL_BALLS is honored for scripted escalation runs
Release pycro evidence confirms >60 wall_fps stability for 3000 and 6000 in short auto sessions
Headless pygame short-protocol run at 3000/5s produced ~55.84 wall_fps summary, below the 57 stable threshold
Direct pygame runs under Python 3.14.2 + pygame-ce 2.5.7 (SDL 2.32.10) at 25000/5s reported ~40.84-42.58 wall_fps, matching the user-observed 41-43 reference range
Legacy local stack Python 3.9.5 + pygame 2.4.0 (SDL 2.26.4) produced only ~19-20 wall_fps at 25000/5s; stack/version parity is now mandatory benchmark metadata
macroquad direct benchmark at 25000 produced ~112-117 wall_fps in this environment, establishing backend headroom above pycro and pygame-ce
Direct 3-second baseline refresh at 25000: pycro=23.29 wall_fps, pygame-ce(.venv314)=33.37 wall_fps, macroquad_direct=81.16 wall_fps
docs/perf-phase-3-plan.md defines parallel subagent slices, ownership boundaries, and before/after median acceptance criteria for context-reset handoff
Clean kickoff on codex/perf-phase3-clean-start removed stale examples/compare/results* artifacts before baseline capture

Runtime hotspot investigator, benchmark harness auditor, and measurement auditor launched in parallel; consolidated risks flag noisy outliers, protocol drift, and incomplete metadata as patch-blocking risks
Direct 25000/3s baseline is now tracked as 3x per runtime with current summary medians: pycro≈23.83, pygame-ce(.venv314)≈40.00, macroquad_direct≈77.28 wall_fps (pygame includes one noisy outlier run)
One pygame-ce run with elapsed≈3.84 and summary wall_fps=0.00 is explicitly marked noisy and queued for repeat/filter per protocol
Optimization slice S1 on runtime submit_render reduced command-name allocation and removed temporary ops vector during batch enqueue (rollback preserved on parse error)

S1 quick direct pycro rerun at 25000/3s (3x) reported summary wall_fps 24.16 / 24.07 / 23.86 (median≈24.07), a provisional uplift over clean-start summary median≈23.83; full acceptance remains gated by sample-normalized scoring and guardrails

Benchmark summary bug fix applied on pycro+pygame compare scripts so summary wall_fps/sim_fps uses full-session accumulators, eliminating false 0.00 summaries at 3000/6000 when per-second counters reset near timeout
Phase-3 quick comparison protocol now uses direct 25000-ball runs with 2.5-second sessions and 2 runs per candidate for faster before/after decisions

Optimization slice S2 in runtime added QueuedDrawBatch CircleRun, Arc-backed submit_render command caching, cached-position Vec2 fast path for draw_circle, cached lifecycle callables for setup/update, and configurable update-time stdio flushing defaulting off
Latest slice-2 pycro direct summaries at 25000 measured wall_fps 25.43 / 25.09 / 25.06 / 24.57 versus a prior baseline band around 22.5-23.4 wall_fps
Direct freeze protocol rerun (25000 balls, 2.5s, 2 runs) now records medians pycro≈24.53, pygame-ce(.venv314)≈40.24, macroquad_direct≈78.64 with stack metadata attached
Guardrail reruns for pycro (3000 and 6000 balls, 2 runs each) stayed above 110 wall_fps medians and showed no >5% regression signal in this sample
Phase-compare-benchmark closeout recorded after direct 25000/2.5s/2-run baselines and 3000/6000 guardrail reruns were captured with stack metadata
Closeout proposals captured in docs/perf-phase-3-closeout.md include extending vector->sprite optimizations to non-circle geometric primitives via shared options contract
tasks.next_action: Closed. Continue primitive-optimization experiments under phase-5 objective tasks and reopen compare stream only if protocol/contracts change.

parallel_streams.title: Compare pycro vs pygame bouncing-ball throughput without FPS drop
parallel_streams.objective: Implement equivalent pycro and pygame benchmark scripts with identical controls and per-second FPS sampling
success_evidence: Both scripts support Left/Right ball-count changes in steps of 50 and random-color bouncing balls
HUD shows balls, fps sampled per second, target_fps, and best stable balls
Benchmark protocol and results table template are documented under examples/compare
follow_through_order: freeze direct baseline summaries at 25000 with 2.5-second sessions and 2 runs
run parallel optimization slices (runtime/backend, benchmark harness, measurement auditor)
persist before/after evidence in tracker/state and keep only positive-median patches
close stream after protocol/evidence baseline is captured and synchronized in tracker/state
